validationId.description: Unique L1 validation ID used network-wide to identify L1 validation until its weight is reduced to 0 i.e. removed.

validationIdHex.description: Unique L1 validation ID used network-wide to identify L1 validation until its weight is reduced to 0 i.e. removed. In hex format

weight.description: Weight of the L1 validator used while sampling validators within the L1. A zero-weight L1 validator means it has been removed from the L1, and the validationID is no longer valid

remainingBalance.description: Remaining L1 validator balance in nAVAX until inactive. It can rejoin L1 sampling by increasing balance with IncreaseL1ValidatorBalanceTx

balanceChange.description: The increase in L1 validator balance (nAVAX) in the current transaction. When the balance is returned after the L1 validator is disabled or removed, this value is negative
